(SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING) PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacatedefault Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-28-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT OLAOLUWA MAKANJUOLA'S Motion To Vacate Default Judgment.

Judgment debtor Olaoluwa J. Makanjuola's unopposed motion to vacate default judgment is granted. Makanjuola provides a declaration that he never received actual notice of this lawsuit until the judgment creditor sought to garnish his wages. Judgment creditor has filed no opposition. The court concludes there is good cause to vacate the judgment pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, section 473, subdivision (d).

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Dec-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A.'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. The Judgment creditor withdrew its opposition to the claim of exemption on 12/9/25. The court will issue an order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT OLAOLUWA MAKANJUOLA'S AMENDED MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT JUDGMENT.

Due to the court's unavailability on December 22, 2025, this case is called on December 23, 2025.

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar per the court's minutes of December 3, 2025 due to insufficient notice for the motion. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-03-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT OLAOLUWA MAKANJUOLA'S Motion To Vacate Default Judgment.

The motion to vacate default judgment is ordered off calendar. Moving party has not filed a proof of service of the motion on the opposing party as required by California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1300, subdivision (c). In addition, a party must provide notice of a motion hearing at least 16 court days in advance of the hearing, with additional time allotted where the moving papers are served by email, mail, or overnight delivery. See Code of Civil Procedure, section 1005, subdivision (b), and section 1010.6, subdivision (a)(3)(B).

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
